text: Common o-vinylphenols undergo a formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition to alkynes using just a catalytic amount of the rhodium pentalenyl precursor [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2, in a process involving activation of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl component to produce directly useful benzoxepines in a straightforward, convenient and extremely efficient manner. By using CO instead of an alkyne, coumarins result via a formally (5 + 1) cycloaddition process.
